---
title: "Chapter 4: Measuring Spread"
output: html_document
---

# Measuring Spread

An average doesn't tell the whole story. We also need to understand how much the data varies around that central point.

## Variance and Standard Deviation

Sample variance $s^2$ computes the average squared deviation from the mean:

$$s^2 = \frac{1}{n-1} \sum_{i=1}^{n} (X_i - \bar{X})^2$$

We can compute both variance and standard deviation directly in R:

```{r variance-calc}
coffee_cups <- c(3, 4, 2, 5, 4)
var_cups <- var(coffee_cups)
sd_cups <- sd(coffee_cups)
cat("Variance:", var_cups, "\nStandard Deviation:", sd_cups)
